source_scope: Prior-art sweep for Semantic Planning & Valid-State Learning (SPV0). Covers explicit semantic planning, structured prediction over programs, set-based object detection, retrieval-and-edit, structured energy networks, sequence-level distillation, DAgger, and discrete flow matching. Academic metadata verified against canonical arXiv/venue records; entries already committed in other source manifests or research-lineage.md are deliberately omitted unless SPV0 adds a distinct framing.

sources.title: Pointer Networks
sources.summary: Neural architecture that outputs a sequence of pointers to input positions, enabling variable-size output dictionaries for ordered set selection.

metadata.feedback_claim: Pointer-style output can express role-slot to symbol bindings without fixing output vocabulary size.
metadata.repo_relevance: SPV0 binding pointer candidates and ordered slot filling
metadata.limitations: No OpenUI grammar constraint or compiler legality integration; training recipe not reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Binding pointers constrained to live compiler symbols reduce invalid reference errors compared to open-vocabulary generation.

sources.title: Abstract Syntax Networks
sources.summary: Modular decoder that emits program structure via typed constructors and syntax constraints, separating topology from terminal values.

metadata.feedback_claim: Program generation can be factored into structural decisions and terminal fills.
metadata.limitations: Autoregressive decoder; no masked-diffusion or exact-state plan conditioning.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: A factored plan IR lets the same decoder vary plan provenance (gold, predicted, retrieved) without changing the grammar transition.

sources.title: Set Transformer: A Framework for Attention-based Permutation-Invariant Neural Networks
sources.summary: Attention-based architecture for set-structured inputs that is permutation-invariant and can produce set-structured outputs.

metadata.feedback_claim: Set attention can encode unordered collections of roles, symbols, and bindings.
metadata.limitations: No OpenUI-specific plan vocabulary or compiler-state conditioning.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Permutation-invariant plan encodings align better with order-insensitive OpenUI component sets than sequential position embeddings.

sources.title: DETR: End-to-End Object Detection with Transformers
sources.summary: Set-prediction approach to object detection using bipartite matching between predictions and ground-truth objects.

metadata.feedback_claim: Set prediction with bipartite matching can supervise variable-cardinality structure without fixed ordering.
metadata.repo_relevance: SPV0 role-slot cardinality and set-matching losses
metadata.limitations: Detection supervision does not transfer directly to program-plan training.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Hungarian matching over predicted versus gold role-slot sets removes the need to impose an arbitrary canonical order on OpenUI components.

sources.title: Diffusion On Syntax Trees
sources.summary: Discrete diffusion model that corrupts and denoises abstract syntax trees rather than token sequences.

metadata.feedback_claim: Tree-structured diffusion can preserve syntactic validity during refinement.
metadata.limitations: No OpenUI grammar or plan-provenance integration; exact training recipe not reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Plan-conditioned topology edits restricted by the compiler's exact legal action set avoid invalid intermediate structures.

sources.title: Retrieve-and-Edit: A Framework for Predicting Structured Outputs
sources.summary: Retrieves a similar example and edits it into a new structured output, using retrieval as a soft prior.

metadata.feedback_claim: Retrieved prototypes can provide a soft initial plan that is later edited.
metadata.limitations: No repository retrieval index or edit-distance learner is implemented.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Retrieved plans are safe as soft seeds or scorer features but must not be treated as hard constraints.

sources.title: Structured Prediction Energy Networks
sources.summary: Defines a scalar energy over complete structured outputs and learns by contrasting compatible and incompatible structures.

metadata.feedback_claim: A global energy can rank whole plans against hard constraints without local greedy decisions.
metadata.repo_relevance: SPV0 program energy and global ranker regret
metadata.limitations: No SPEN inference or training recipe is reproduced.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Global plan energy over compiler-verified terminals measures preference without weakening legality checks.

sources.title: Sequence-Level Knowledge Distillation
sources.summary: Distills a large teacher into a smaller model by training on the teacher's sequence-level outputs.

metadata.feedback_claim: Dense teacher distributions over structured outputs can supervise a small plan model.
metadata.repo_relevance: SPV0 dense teacher supervision and plan-factor distillation
metadata.limitations: No plan-specific teacher or distillation objective is implemented.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Teacher distributions conditioned on exact compiler state provide more useful plan supervision than sequence-level KD alone.

sources.title: A Reduction of Imitation Learning and Structured Prediction to No-Regret Online Learning
sources.summary: DAgger reduces imitation learning to no-regret online learning by aggregating on-policy training data.

metadata.feedback_claim: On-policy aggregation can improve a learned plan policy beyond single-oracle supervised learning.
metadata.repo_relevance: SPV0 DAgger-style plan arm and solver-state supervision
metadata.limitations: No DAgger loop or on-policy plan oracle is implemented.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: DAgger-style aggregation over plan-conditioned decode trajectories can reduce compounding errors when the plan is only a soft guide.

sources.title: Constrained Code Generation with Discrete Diffusion
sources.summary: Uses program analysis and optimization to localize and steer constraint-relevant regions of global diffusion states.

metadata.feedback_claim: Constraint-aware localization can steer diffusion while preserving global structure.
metadata.limitations: No CDC optimizer or OpenUI-specific constraint localization is implemented.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Plan-derived localization is safe only when routed through the compiler's exact legal action set.

sources.title: Discrete Flow Matching
sources.summary: Generalizes continuous flow matching to discrete state spaces with straight paths between probability distributions.

metadata.feedback_claim: Discrete flow models can express direct transitions between structured states.
metadata.limitations: No discrete flow model or OpenUI path distribution is implemented.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Plan-conditioned valid edits that stay inside the compiler's legal action set form a discrete flow between semantically related programs.

sources.title: FS-DFM: Few-Step Discrete Flow Models for Language Modeling
sources.summary: Reduces discrete flow sampling to a few solver steps while maintaining generation quality.

metadata.feedback_claim: Few-step discrete flows can make plan-conditioned editing practical at decode time.
metadata.limitations: No FS-DFM sampler or OpenUI edit path model is implemented.
metadata.novel_hypothesis: Few-step plan edits constrained by exact compiler state may achieve lower latency than full remasking while preserving validity.
